operators.what_it_is: Creates or modifies geometry by adding points and building polygons procedurally.
operators.mental_model: A geometry builder that can place dots in space and connect them into shapes.
operators.example: Generate a line of points along a path and automatically connect them into a polygonal rail.

Points.PointStat: Uses statistics or external data to define point creation.
Points.NamedAttributes: Uses named attributes as sources for point data.
Points.DeleteGeometry: Removes incoming geometry before adding new points.
Points.KeepPoints: Preserves existing points while adding new ones.
Points.Position: Defines the position of added points.
Points.Weight: Controls weighting or influence of points.

Polygon.Method: Determines how polygons are constructed from points.
Polygon.Group: Specifies which point group is used for polygon creation.
Polygon.Add: Adds polygons based on current settings.
Polygon.Close Polygon: Closes the polygon by connecting last point to first.
Polygon.Table: Uses a DAT table to define polygon structure.
Polygon.Polygon Pattern: Defines a repeating or patterned polygon structure.

Post.RemoveUnusedPoints: Deletes points not used by any primitive.
Post.ComputeNormals: Generates surface normals after geometry creation.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It puts dots in space and draws lines between them to make shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Imports geometry from Alembic cache files into TouchDesigner.
operators.mental_model: A time-aware loader for baked 3D scenes.
operators.example: Load a cached character animation exported from Maya for playback and processing.

File.Alembic File: Path to the Alembic cache file.
File.Object Path: Path inside the Alembic hierarchy to load.
File.FPS: Frames per second for time evaluation.
File.Direct to GPU: Uploads geometry directly to the GPU.
File.Unload: Releases the Alembic file from memory.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It plays back a saved 3D movie of shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Aligns and offsets geometry or UVs based on bounding rules.
operators.mental_model: A ruler that lines things up exactly where you want.
operators.example: Center multiple meshes or align UV islands consistently.

Align.Align and Bias Left UV: Aligns and biases UVs to the left.
Align.Right UV End: Aligns UVs to the right boundary.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lines shapes up neatly like blocks on a shelf.

operators.what_it_is: Creates and controls an inverse-kinematics arm rig.
operators.mental_model: A bendy robot arm that knows where its hand should go.
operators.example: Pose a character arm by dragging the hand target.
operators.explain_like_im_five: You move the hand, and the arm bends to follow.

operators.what_it_is: Deletes or renames geometry attributes at different levels.
operators.mental_model: A label eraser and relabeler for geometry data.
operators.example: Remove unused point attributes before exporting geometry.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It changes or removes the names stuck to parts of a shape.

operators.what_it_is: Creates new geometry attributes or computes derived attributes like normals and tangents.
operators.mental_model: A calculator that writes new data onto points, vertices, or faces.
operators.example: Add custom point attributes or generate normals for lighting.

Attribute Create.Attribute Create: Creates a new attribute with specified values.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It adds new information to shapes, like arrows that show which way they face.

operators.what_it_is: Generates and manipulates curve bases and parameterized directions.
operators.mental_model: A math ruler that defines directions along a curve.
operators.example: Create U and V directions along a surface for deformation.

U.Edit the U: Enables editing of the U basis.
U.Raise To: Raises U values by a power.

V.Edit the V: Enables editing of the V basis.
V.Raise To: Raises V values by a power.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It tells shapes how to count along a line in two directions.

operators.what_it_is: Blends multiple geometry inputs together based on weights.
operators.mental_model: A crossfader for shapes.
operators.example: Morph one mesh smoothly into another.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It mixes shapes together like sliding a volume knob.

operators.what_it_is: Groups bones for skeletal deformation constraints.
operators.mental_model: A rulebook for how many bones can affect a point.
operators.example: Limit character skinning to a fixed number of bones.

Bone Group.Max Bones per Group: Maximum bones allowed in a group.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It decides how many bones are allowed to pull on a shape.

operators.what_it_is: Combines or subtracts geometry using boolean operations.
operators.mental_model: Cookie cutters for 3D shapes.
operators.example: Cut windows out of a wall mesh.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It cuts, adds, or overlaps shapes like clay.

operators.what_it_is: Generates a box primitive with configurable orientation, size, and topology.
operators.mental_model: A parametric cardboard box you can resize, rotate, and subdivide.
operators.example: Create a modular building block for architectural layouts.

Box.Orient: Sets the orientation of the box.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a box you can stretch and turn.

operators.what_it_is: Connects separate edge groups by generating faces between them.
operators.mental_model: A zipper that stitches two edges together.
operators.example: Bridge the gap between two open mesh borders.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It fills the gap between two edges.

operators.what_it_is: Creates surfaces from curves with controllable smoothing and curvature.
operators.mental_model: Stretching a sheet over curved wires.
operators.example: Generate a smooth surface from profile curves.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It stretches a smooth skin over lines.

operators.what_it_is: Stores geometry over time for playback or reuse.
operators.mental_model: A recorder for shapes over time.
operators.example: Cache a simulation to scrub it back and forth.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It remembers shapes so you can rewind them.

operators.what_it_is: Closes open surfaces or curves by generating caps.
operators.mental_model: Putting lids on open ends.
operators.example: Cap the ends of an open tube surface.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It closes the open ends of a shape.

operators.what_it_is: Captures geometry over time or by region and optionally saves capture data to files.
operators.mental_model: A net that grabs geometry data and can store it for later use.
operators.example: Capture deformation data from an animated mesh and save selected points to disk.

Capture.Wait From Capture Frame: Delays capture starting from a specified frame.
Capture.Point Coloring: Colors points based on capture state.

Override.Save File: Saves capture data to a file.
Override.Save All Data to File: Writes all captured data to disk.
Override.Save Selected Points to File: Writes only selected points to disk.

Capture Region.Top Cap: Caps the top of the region.
Capture Region.Bottom Cap: Caps the bottom of the region.

Display.Display Color: Color used to display capture visualization.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It grabs parts of a shape and remembers them.

operators.what_it_is: Cuts curves or surfaces by parameter ranges and extraction rules.
operators.mental_model: A sliding window that slices parts out of a surface or curve.
operators.example: Extract a segment of a NURBS surface between two U values.

Method.Keep Inside: Keeps geometry inside the carve range.
Method.Keep Outside: Keeps geometry outside the carve range.
Method.Cut at all Interval U Breakpoints: Cuts at all U interval breakpoints.
Method.Cut at all Interval V Breakpoints: Cuts at all V interval breakpoints.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It cuts out just the part of a shape you want.

operators.what_it_is: Maps CHOP channel data onto geometry attributes.
operators.mental_model: A translator that turns numbers into shape changes.
operators.example: Drive point positions using animation channels.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It uses moving numbers to wiggle shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Creates circular or arc-based primitives with configurable orientation and resolution.
operators.mental_model: A compass that draws perfect or partial circles in space.
operators.example: Generate a circular profile to be used for sweeping a tube.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It draws a circle or part of a circle.

operators.what_it_is: Deforms geometry as if it were soft material being pushed or pulled.
operators.mental_model: Hands squishing and shaping soft clay.
operators.example: Create organic dents and bulges on a mesh.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets you squish shapes like soft clay.

operators.what_it_is: Cuts geometry using a plane and keeps selected sides.
operators.mental_model: A slicing plane that cuts shapes in half.
operators.example: Slice a model and keep only the top portion.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It cuts a shape and keeps one side.

operators.what_it_is: Converts geometry between different primitive types.
operators.mental_model: Changing one kind of shape math into another.
operators.example: Convert NURBS surfaces into polygons for rendering.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns one kind of shape into another.

operators.what_it_is: Duplicates geometry using templates, points, or transforms.
operators.mental_model: A stamp machine that repeats shapes.
operators.example: Scatter copies of an object onto points.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes lots of copies of a shape.

operators.what_it_is: Loads and executes a custom C++ SOP plugin inside TouchDesigner.
operators.mental_model: A doorway that lets your own compiled code become an operator.
operators.example: Run a custom geometry deformation written in C++.

C++.Plugin Path: Filesystem path to the compiled SOP plugin.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets TouchDesigner use your own code as a tool.

operators.what_it_is: Gradually moves geometry along a surface or direction over time.
operators.mental_model: A slow crawl that slides shapes across space.
operators.example: Make points slowly creep along a surface.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes shapes slowly crawl forward.

operators.what_it_is: Deforms curves and surfaces interactively with localized control.
operators.mental_model: Pushing and smoothing clay along drawn lines.
operators.example: Sculpt a curve directly on a surface.
operators.explain_like_im_five: You draw on a shape and it bends where you touch it.

operators.what_it_is: Offsets geometry based on projection or displacement rules.
operators.mental_model: Pushing geometry outward using invisible forces.
operators.example: Displace a surface using profile-based offsets.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It pushes parts of a shape in or out.

operators.what_it_is: Analyzes curve/surface relationships and can extract intersection-related results.
operators.mental_model: A geometry inspector that compares one set of pieces against another and reports the cuts.
operators.example: Check where cutter curves intersect faces and keep only the resulting side pieces.

Check.Curve Set: Selects the curve set being analyzed.
Check.Left Face: Defines which side is treated as left.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It checks where shapes touch or cross and tells you what happened.

operators.what_it_is: Builds geometry from a DAT table by mapping columns to points, vertices, primitives, and detail attributes.
operators.mental_model: A spreadsheet that turns rows into geometry parts.
operators.example: Generate point clouds and attributes from a table of positions and weights.

DAT to.PointStat: Uses point statistics or point mapping behavior.
DAT to.Add Float Attributes: Adds float attributes from DAT columns.
DAT to.Add Int Attributes: Adds integer attributes from DAT columns.
DAT to.Add String Attributes: Adds string attributes from DAT columns.
DAT to.Particle Type: Specifies particle type when emitting particles.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns a table of numbers into a shape.

operators.what_it_is: Applies deformation using capture/rig data and can manage capture-related attributes.
operators.mental_model: A puppet system that moves geometry using rig rules.
operators.example: Deform a skinned mesh using a skeleton and stored capture weights.

Deform.Deform Normals: Deforms or recomputes normals with the geometry.
Deform.Skeleton Root Path: Root path used to locate the skeleton.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It bends the shape using the rules from a rig.

operators.what_it_is: Deletes or keeps geometry elements based on groups, patterns, numbering, bounds, and directional tests.
operators.mental_model: A bouncer that decides what stays and what gets removed.
operators.example: Delete everything outside a bounding volume or remove unused groups and geometry.

Delete.Delete Unused Groups: Removes groups that no longer contain members.
Delete.Keep Points: Keeps points even if attached elements are deleted.

Normal.Backspace From: Offset/backspace reference for the normal test.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It removes the parts you don’t want.

operators.what_it_is: Subdivides and re-builds polygons, including triangulation and topology refinement options.
operators.mental_model: A geometry re-cutter that can split faces into smaller, more useful faces.
operators.example: Triangulate a mesh and remove shared edges for a more uniform surface.

Divide.Bricker Polygons: Converts polygons into a bricked pattern.
Divide.Remove Shared Edges: Removes edges shared between adjacent polygons.
Divide.Compute Dual: Computes the dual of the mesh.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It cuts big faces into smaller faces in smarter ways.

operators.what_it_is: Extrudes geometry to add thickness or depth and can generate front/back/side meshes and groups.
operators.mental_model: Pulling a flat shape into a 3D solid.
operators.example: Extrude a profile curve to create a thick panel with side walls.

Extrude.Extrude Source Group: Group used as the source for extrusion.

Values.Polygonal Sides: Uses polygonal sides instead of smooth.
Values.Consolidate Faces to Mesh: Consolidates faces into a single mesh.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It takes a flat shape and makes it thick.

operators.what_it_is: Uses CHOP-driven tracking data to drive or align geometry, with GPU and preprocessing options.
operators.mental_model: A tracker hookup that lets motion data steer a mesh.
operators.example: Drive a face mesh using a Face Track CHOP stream.

Face Track.Pre-Transform Mesh: Applies a pre-transform to the mesh.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It uses tracking data to move a face-shaped mesh.

operators.what_it_is: Edits mesh topology and normals, including cusp, unique points, consolidation, and cleanup operations.
operators.mental_model: A mesh fixer that can sharpen edges or smooth shading.
operators.example: Cusp polygons for hard edges and remove degenerate geometry.

Facet.Unique Points: Splits shared points to make them unique.
Facet.Cusp Polygons: Creates hard edges by splitting normals.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It fixes a mesh and decides whether edges look sharp or smooth.

operators.what_it_is: Loads geometry from a file and controls primitive interpretation and normal generation.
operators.mental_model: A file loader for 3D shapes.
operators.example: Load an OBJ/FBX mesh and compute normals if missing.

File In.File Primitive Faces: How primitive faces are interpreted from the file.
File In.Compute Normals if None Exists: Computes normals only if the file has none.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It opens a saved 3D shape file.

operators.what_it_is: Rounds or bevels edges and curves by inserting smooth transitional geometry.
operators.mental_model: A corner-rounder that softens sharp edges.
operators.example: Add rounded corners to a hard-edged model for smoother highlights.

Fillet.Fillet Group: Group of edges or primitives to fillet.
Fillet.Wrap Last to First: Wraps fillet from last element to first.
Fillet.Primitive Type: Primitive type used for fillet geometry.
Fillet.Match Input to Fillets: Matches input geometry to fillet output.
Fillet.Cut Primitives: Cuts original primitives at fillet locations.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It rounds off sharp corners so they aren’t pointy.

operators.what_it_is: Fits curves or surfaces to new parameterizations and continuity rules.
operators.mental_model: A reshaper that makes curves behave more evenly.
operators.example: Refit a curve to have smoother parameter spacing.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It redraws a curve so it’s smoother and more even.

operators.what_it_is: Applies procedural force fields to geometry.
operators.mental_model: Invisible pushes and pulls acting on a shape.
operators.example: Apply a directional or spiral force to deform points.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It pushes or pulls a shape like wind or magnetism.

operators.what_it_is: Adds fractal-based displacement or detail to geometry.
operators.mental_model: Noise that makes shapes rough and natural.
operators.example: Add rocky, uneven detail to a surface.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes shapes bumpy and natural-looking.

operators.what_it_is: Generates a planar grid with controllable resolution, orientation, and UVs.
operators.mental_model: Graph paper laid out in 3D space.
operators.example: Create a ground plane with evenly spaced rows and columns.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a flat checkerboard of squares.

operators.what_it_is: Creates and manages geometry groups using rules, patterns, bounds, and directional tests.
operators.mental_model: Sticky notes that label parts of a shape.
operators.example: Create groups for edges facing upward or points inside a box.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets you tag parts of a shape so you can use them later.

operators.what_it_is: Edits group membership and group definitions, including renaming, converting, and destroying groups.
operators.mental_model: An eraser and rename tool for group labels.
operators.example: Rename a group or remove it after use.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It changes or removes group names on a shape.

operators.what_it_is: Identifies and processes holes in polygonal geometry, optionally snapping them to outlines.
operators.mental_model: Finding gaps in a mesh and deciding how they should be treated.
operators.example: Detect open holes in a mesh before filling or bridging them.

Hole.Angle Tolerance: Angular tolerance when evaluating hole edges.
Hole.Snap Holes to Outline: Snaps detected holes to an outline.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It finds holes in a shape and helps line them up.

operators.what_it_is: Selects and imports geometry from parent or external operator hierarchies with animation handling.
operators.mental_model: A selective doorway that pulls in shapes and motion from elsewhere.
operators.example: Import animated geometry from a parent COMP while controlling playback.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It brings in shapes from somewhere else and plays their motion.

operators.what_it_is: Defines an input connector for SOP networks, controlling order and labeling.
operators.mental_model: An input plug that lets geometry flow into a network.
operators.example: Label and order incoming geometry connections.

In.Label: Label for the input.
In.Connect Order: Order in which inputs are connected.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It’s where shapes come into the network.

operators.what_it_is: Generates inverse mappings from curves, often driven by CHOP data.
operators.mental_model: Figuring out where a point would land if you ran a curve backward.
operators.example: Map CHOP values back onto a curve’s parameter space.

Inverse Curve.CHOP: CHOP used as the driving source.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It runs along a curve in reverse to find positions.

operators.what_it_is: Extracts a surface from an implicit volumetric function.
operators.mental_model: Finding the skin of a cloud of math.
operators.example: Create a blobby surface from an implicit field.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It finds the outside skin of an invisible blob.

operators.what_it_is: Builds jointed geometry along curves or paths with control over twist, orientation, and scaling.
operators.mental_model: Placing bendable joints along a hose.
operators.example: Create evenly spaced joints along a spine curve for deformation.

Joint.Orient Circle: Orients joints using a circular frame.
Joint.Align Major Axis: Aligns joints to the major axis.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It puts bendy joints along a line.

operators.what_it_is: Connects geometry to external hardware or sensor-driven skeleton data.
operators.mental_model: A live cable from sensors to shapes.
operators.example: Drive a mesh using a depth sensor skeleton.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets sensors move a shape.

operators.what_it_is: Deforms geometry using a lattice-based control structure.
operators.mental_model: A flexible cage you push to bend what’s inside.
operators.example: Warp a mesh smoothly by adjusting lattice points.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It bends shapes by pushing a soft cage.

operators.what_it_is: Limits geometry attributes using CHOP channels, including transforms and color.
operators.mental_model: Guard rails that stop values from going too far.
operators.example: Clamp rotations and colors driven by animation channels.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It stops numbers from going past the edges.

operators.what_it_is: Creates custom deformation or control behavior driven by user-defined channels.
operators.mental_model: A build-your-own control panel for shapes.
operators.example: Drive deformation using four custom channels.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets you make your own rules for moving shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Creates a straight line primitive defined by two points or a set number of points.
operators.mental_model: A ruler that draws a straight line between two dots.
operators.example: Draw a guide line between two positions in space.

Line.Number of Points: Number of points along the line.
Line.Texture Coordinates: Generates texture coordinates along the line.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It draws a straight line from here to there.

operators.what_it_is: Generates a thickened line or ribbon from a curve or line.
operators.mental_model: Turning a thin line into a strip or band.
operators.example: Create a ribbon-like trail from a path.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a skinny line into a wide strip.

operators.what_it_is: Reduces geometry complexity based on distance or thresholds.
operators.mental_model: A detail knob that turns down shapes when they’re far away.
operators.example: Automatically simplify distant meshes to improve performance.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes shapes simpler when you don’t need detail.

operators.what_it_is: Generates geometry using an L-system procedural growth model.
operators.mental_model: Growing plants using rules and instructions.
operators.example: Create branching tree-like structures procedurally.

LS System.Image File: Image used as a rule source.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It grows shapes like plants using rules.

operators.what_it_is: Deforms geometry by pulling or pushing points toward a magnetic influence region.
operators.mental_model: A magnet that tugs on nearby parts of a shape.
operators.example: Pull points toward a target area to create dents or bulges.

Magnet.Deform Group: Group of geometry affected by the magnet.
Magnet.Magnet Group: Group defining the magnetic influence region.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It pulls parts of a shape like a magnet.

operators.what_it_is: Assigns or manages material-related attributes on geometry.
operators.mental_model: A tag that tells shapes what they’re made of.
operators.example: Assign a material attribute before rendering.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It tells a shape what it should look like.

operators.what_it_is: Combines multiple SOP inputs into a single geometry stream.
operators.mental_model: A funnel that pours many shapes into one.
operators.example: Merge several meshes into a single output.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It puts shapes together.

operators.what_it_is: Creates blobby implicit surfaces that merge smoothly based on proximity.
operators.mental_model: Blobs of goo that melt together when close.
operators.example: Generate organic blobby forms that fuse together.

Metaball.Center Weight: Weight at the center of the metaball.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes blobby shapes that melt together.

operators.what_it_is: Provides a placeholder or reference model geometry.
operators.mental_model: A simple stand-in shape.
operators.example: Use a default model as a reference in a network.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It gives you a basic shape to work with.

operators.what_it_is: Applies procedural noise to geometry attributes for variation and deformation.
operators.mental_model: Random bumps and wiggles added to a shape.
operators.example: Add organic surface variation using noise.

Noise.Attribute Type: Attribute the noise is applied to.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes shapes bumpy and uneven.

operators.what_it_is: Passes geometry through unchanged and is used for organization and reference.
operators.mental_model: A labeled checkpoint in a geometry network.
operators.example: Mark a stable output to reference later in the network.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It does nothing, on purpose.

operators.what_it_is: Brings geometry from other objects or SOPs into the current SOP network.
operators.mental_model: A vacuum hose that pulls shapes in from elsewhere.
operators.example: Merge geometry from another Geo COMP while controlling transforms.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It grabs shapes from somewhere else.

operators.what_it_is: Imports geometry and tracking models from OpenVR devices.
operators.mental_model: A bridge between VR hardware and geometry.
operators.example: Load a VR controller model into the scene.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It brings VR stuff into TouchDesigner.

operators.what_it_is: Defines the output of a SOP network.
operators.mental_model: The exit door for shapes.
operators.example: Choose which SOP is the final output of a Geo.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It says which shape comes out.

operators.what_it_is: Simulates particle motion and lifecycle based on rules and forces.
operators.mental_model: A sandbox full of moving dots with physics rules.
operators.example: Emit particles from a surface and let them drift and collide.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes lots of tiny dots move around.

operators.what_it_is: Edits point-level attributes such as position, color, normals, and particle-related data.
operators.mental_model: A point editor that decides which data to keep or modify on each dot.
operators.example: Move points while preserving their color, normals, and particle velocity.

Point.Point Group: Group of points to operate on.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It changes dots but lets you choose what parts stay the same.

operators.what_it_is: Creates polygon surfaces by lofting between input curves or profiles.
operators.mental_model: Stretching fabric between a stack of wires.
operators.example: Loft multiple profile curves into a continuous surface.

Polyloft.Closes Ends: Closes the start and end of the loft.
Polyloft.Create Polygon Group: Creates a group for generated polygons.
Polyloft.Name: Name of the created group.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It stretches a surface between lines.

operators.what_it_is: Creates or manages point-based groups.
operators.mental_model: Putting selected dots into named buckets.
operators.example: Group specific points for later deformation.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It puts dots into named groups.

operators.what_it_is: Builds polygon patches from curves with continuity and clamping controls.
operators.mental_model: Sewing a patch of fabric with controlled edges.
operators.example: Create a smooth polygon patch from boundary curves.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a flat patch from curved edges.

operators.what_it_is: Reduces polygon count while preserving important features.
operators.mental_model: Shrinking a mesh without losing its shape.
operators.example: Optimize a dense mesh for real-time rendering.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a shape simpler without ruining it.

operators.what_it_is: Converts polygonal geometry into spline-based curves with controllable continuity and division.
operators.mental_model: Redrawing a jagged line as a smooth curve using rules.
operators.example: Turn polygon outlines into smooth splines for lofting.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns straight edges into smooth lines.

operators.what_it_is: Stitches adjacent polygons together by merging edges and points.
operators.mental_model: Sewing fabric pieces together along their edges.
operators.example: Stitch separate polygon shells into one continuous surface.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It sews shapes together along the edges.

operators.what_it_is: Interfaces SOP geometry with POP-style particle data workflows.
operators.mental_model: A bridge between particles and shapes.
operators.example: Pull particle data into a SOP-based workflow.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It lets particle data become shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Creates or modifies primitives using source and template geometry, with transform and attribute controls.
operators.mental_model: Building faces using rules and templates.
operators.example: Generate primitives based on a template and apply attributes.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It builds faces and gives them properties.

operators.what_it_is: Generates hull-like surfaces from faces with U/V preservation and clamping controls.
operators.mental_model: Wrapping a tight skin around faces.
operators.example: Create a hull surface that respects original face flow.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It wraps faces in a smooth shell.

operators.what_it_is: Builds implicit meta-surfaces from weighted inputs.
operators.mental_model: Invisible blobs that blend together based on weight.
operators.example: Generate a meta-surface from multiple weighted sources.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes blobby shapes that merge smoothly.

operators.what_it_is: Controls how particle geometry is represented and rendered.
operators.mental_model: Choosing how dots should look.
operators.example: Render particles as sprites or geometry.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It decides how particles are drawn.

operators.what_it_is: Extracts or generates profile curves from surfaces with precise parameter mapping.
operators.mental_model: Tracing clean guide lines along a surface.
operators.example: Pull profile curves off a surface to use for sweeping or lofting.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It pulls neat lines off a surface.

operators.what_it_is: Projects curves or profiles onto surfaces using ray or vector-based methods.
operators.mental_model: Shining a flashlight to cast lines onto a surface.
operators.example: Project a circle profile onto a curved surface.

Project.Circle Type: Type of circle used for projection.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It sticks lines onto a surface.

operators.what_it_is: Sweeps cross-sections along one or more rail curves to generate surfaces.
operators.mental_model: Sliding a shape along tracks.
operators.example: Sweep a profile along paired rails to form a complex surface.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It slides a shape along rails to make a surface.

operators.what_it_is: Downloads or converts raster-based data into SOP geometry context.
operators.mental_model: Turning image-like data into geometry-friendly form.
operators.example: Use raster data as a source for geometry operations.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It brings image-style data into shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Casts rays from geometry to intersect other surfaces and generate intersection data.
operators.mental_model: Shooting invisible lasers and seeing where they hit.
operators.example: Project points onto another mesh and record hit distance.

Ray.Ray Hit Group: Creates a group for hit points.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It shoots rays and finds where they hit.

operators.what_it_is: Creates a rectangular primitive with optional rounding and camera-based sizing.
operators.mental_model: A flat card you can size, round, and aim at a camera.
operators.example: Create a rounded UI panel aligned to a camera’s aspect.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a flat rectangle you can round.

operators.what_it_is: Refines curves or surfaces by subdividing specific parameter ranges.
operators.mental_model: Adding more detail only where you need it.
operators.example: Refine a NURBS surface locally to increase detail.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It adds more points where things need detail.

operators.what_it_is: Resamples curves or edges to control point distribution and segment length.
operators.mental_model: Re-spacing beads evenly on a string.
operators.example: Evenly space points along a curve for animation.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It spaces points evenly along a line.

operators.what_it_is: Resolves geometry connectivity and can revolve profiles into surfaces.
operators.mental_model: Cleaning up paths and spinning them into shapes.
operators.example: Resolve connectivity and revolve a profile into a surface.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It fixes paths and can spin them into shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Generates or modifies geometry using scripting callbacks.
operators.mental_model: A programmable pen that draws shapes.
operators.example: Procedurally generate geometry using a script.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It uses code to make shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Selects specific SOP inputs for downstream use.
operators.mental_model: Choosing which wire to listen to.
operators.example: Pick one SOP from several inputs.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It picks which shape to use.

operators.what_it_is: Blends a sequence of geometries over time or index.
operators.mental_model: A crossfader that steps through shapes.
operators.example: Blend between multiple mesh states in order.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It fades from one shape to the next.

operators.what_it_is: Builds a surface by skinning through cross-sections, with control over wrapping and polygon output.
operators.mental_model: Stretching a surface over a stack of ribs.
operators.example: Skin multiple profile curves into a continuous surface shell.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a surface by stretching between lines.

operators.what_it_is: Reorders points, primitives, or particles using rules like randomization, offsets, and vector directions.
operators.mental_model: Rearranging a list of geometry elements into a new order.
operators.example: Sort points along a vector direction to prepare for sweeping or instancing.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It changes the order of dots or faces.

operators.what_it_is: Generates a sphere primitive with configurable topology, orientation, and UVs.
operators.mental_model: A parametric ball you can subdivide and aim.
operators.example: Create a high-resolution sphere for lighting tests or deformation.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a ball you can add more lines to.

operators.what_it_is: Simulates spring dynamics on points with forces, constraints, and physical attributes like mass and drag.
operators.mental_model: A net of springs that jiggles and settles.
operators.example: Make a chain of points bounce with spring tension and collision limits.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes points act like they’re connected by rubber bands.

operators.what_it_is: Computes intersection curves between two surface groups and optionally trims results based on inside/outside rules.
operators.mental_model: Finding and cutting along where two surfaces cross each other.
operators.example: Extract clean intersection curves between two NURBS surfaces.

Method.A Profiles Group: Group for profiles created on A.
Method.B Profiles Group: Group for profiles created on B.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It finds where two surfaces touch and cuts along that line.

operators.what_it_is: Sweeps cross-section geometry along a path to create surfaces or solids.
operators.mental_model: Dragging a shape along a track to leave a surface behind.
operators.example: Sweep a circle along a curve to create a tube.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It slides a shape along a line to make a surface.

operators.what_it_is: Selects one of multiple SOP inputs to pass through.
operators.mental_model: A railroad switch choosing which track to use.
operators.example: Toggle between different geometry versions.

Switch.Select Input: Index of the input to output.
Switch.Extend: Behavior when index is out of range.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It chooses which shape gets through.

operators.what_it_is: Generates vector or polygonal text geometry with typographic layout controls.
operators.mental_model: A typesetter that turns words into shapes.
operators.example: Create extruded 3D text with proper alignment and wrapping.

Text.Scale Font to BB Box Height: Scales font to bounding box height.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns words into shapes you can see and touch.

operators.what_it_is: Applies or generates texture coordinates on geometry using various projection methods.
operators.mental_model: Wrapping paper around shapes.
operators.example: Project camera-based UVs onto a mesh.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It tells shapes how pictures stick to them.

operators.what_it_is: Creates a torus (donut-shaped) primitive with detailed topology controls.
operators.mental_model: A donut you can slice into neat rows and columns.
operators.example: Generate a torus with custom wrapping and end caps.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a donut shape.

operators.what_it_is: Converts raster images into vector or polygonal outlines.
operators.mental_model: Drawing around the edges of a picture.
operators.example: Trace a logo image into clean vector geometry.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns pictures into line shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Accumulates previous frames of geometry to create motion trails or history-based geometry.
operators.mental_model: Leaving footprints behind moving shapes.
operators.example: Create streaks behind moving points.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It shows where things have been.

operators.what_it_is: Applies spatial transformations to geometry.
operators.mental_model: Grab, move, turn, and resize a shape.
operators.example: Rotate and scale a mesh around a pivot.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It moves and turns shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Trims surfaces using profile curves with precise tolerance controls.
operators.mental_model: Cutting out parts of a surface with scissors.
operators.example: Trim a NURBS surface using projected curves.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It cuts surfaces using lines.

operators.what_it_is: Reorders triangles into triangle strips for efficiency.
operators.mental_model: Lacing triangles into long zipper-like strips.
operators.example: Optimize triangle meshes for faster rendering.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It lines up triangles into long strips.

operators.what_it_is: Creates a tubular primitive with detailed topology and caps.
operators.mental_model: A pipe you can twist and resize.
operators.example: Generate a hollow tube with end caps.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It makes a pipe shape.

operators.what_it_is: Applies a twisting deformation around an axis.
operators.mental_model: Twisting a towel around its length.
operators.example: Add a corkscrew twist to a column.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It twists shapes like a spiral.

operators.what_it_is: Edits vertex-level attributes independently from points.
operators.mental_model: Adjusting the corners of faces instead of dots.
operators.example: Modify vertex colors without changing point colors.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It changes corner data on shapes.

operators.what_it_is: Converts geometry into a wireframe representation.
operators.mental_model: Turning faces into metal rods.
operators.example: Create a wireframe version of a mesh.
operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns shapes into wires.

operators.what_it_is: Converts ZED camera depth and image data into geometry inside SOPs.
operators.mental_model: Turning a depth camera’s view into actual 3D points and surfaces.
operators.example: Generate live point clouds or meshes from a ZED stereo camera feed.

ZED.ZED TOP: Source ZED TOP providing depth and image data.
ZED.Maximum Memory: Limits memory usage for geometry generation.
ZED.Range: Depth range used for geometry creation.
ZED.Texture: Applies texture coordinates from the camera image.
ZED.Consolidate Points: Merges nearby points to reduce density.

operators.explain_like_im_five: It turns what the depth camera sees into real 3D shapes.
